title: K1c — Queue-steering polish + parity (optional)
description: Optional; evaluate after K1b. Queue-mode parity with Kiro's Ctrl+S toggle: a client-side buffer that flushes to session/prompt on TurnCompleted instead of steering mid-turn (pure cyril-side, no new wire). Subagent steering: probe _session/steer against subagent session ids, then wire /steer @<name> <msg> through SubagentTracker::find_by_name if it works.

title: KAS-0 — Engine trait + v2 port
description: Engine abstraction trait + port of current v2 (Rust) behavior behind it. Will later absorb K1a's three new convert/kiro.rs steering arms into the V2Engine impl at negligible cost. First milestone of the KAS engine integration track.
notes:
Reconstructed 2026-06-17 from .k1a-steering/related-issues.md; detail from ROADMAP KAS track.

Turn-end coupling (from PR #22 / cyril-84ca, 2026-06-17): the off-loop prompt dispatch made TurnCompleted be emitted when conn.prompt().await resolves, and the one-turn busy-guard keys off prompt_task.is_finished(). Both assume 'prompt response == turn end' — true for v2, FALSE for KAS (KAS-2a: turn-end is session_info_update->turn_end; the prompt response is secondary/late). Under KAS, is_finished() stays false after a logical turn-end, so a legitimate next prompt is wrongly rejected with 'a turn is already in progress', and TurnCompleted fires late/never. When this milestone introduces the Engine trait's turn-end detection and ports v2 behind V2Engine, route BOTH the busy-guard and TurnCompleted emission through the engine's turn-end signal, NOT prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await. Concrete code: bridge.rs SendPrompt guard + the spawned prompt_task.

title: K1a — Queue-steering wire + state plumbing
description: Wire + state machinery for Kiro 2.7.0 queue steering. Converter arm for _kiro.dev/session/update (steering_queued/consumed/cleared -> notifications), bridge SteerSession/ClearSteering as awaited _session/steer[/clear] ExtRequests with a -32601 emit-once unsupported gate, and SessionController/UiState mirrors (steering_depth, steering_unsupported, steering_queued). No UX — that is K1b.
notes: Reconstructed 2026-06-17 after the working-tree rivets DB was lost (committed empty on the chore branch). Done: merged to main via PR #18 (a8e1037) + #19 (253ff46) review follow-ups.

title: K1b cross-turn steer echo/chip turn-end semantics unverified (needs backend probe)
description: Code-review (cyril-bm1j, xhigh) found a latent ambiguity in K1b's turn-end handling. TurnCompleted resets the chip (steering_queued=0, claim C9) but leaves any still-Queued SteerEcho in scrollback (negative-space #6). If the backend PERSISTS an end-of-turn-un-consumed steer and drains it on the NEXT turn (the idle-steer capture .k1b-steering/idle-steer-wire-capture.log shows steers survive the idle->turn boundary, suggesting it does), then: (a) the chip UNDER-counts a still-pending steer after turn-end, and (b) the next turn's SteeringConsumed FIFO-flips the stale prior-turn Queued echo. If instead the backend DROPS un-consumed steers at turn-end, the current reset is right but the echo should be finalized too. Both directions bake in an unverified assumption, so the fix was deferred. ACTION: probe whether a steer queued at end-of-turn (no further tool boundary) drains on the next turn; then either (1) stop resetting the chip at turn-end and let it drain naturally, or (2) finalize leftover Queued echoes at turn-end to match the reset. The cross-SESSION variant was already fixed (session_created_finalizes_prior_session_steer_echoes); this is the same mechanism across a TURN boundary within one session.

title: K1b — Queue-steering TUI UX
description: User-facing steering UX on top of K1a plumbing. Enter-while-busy: when session.status()==Busy and input is non-slash text, route submit to SteerSession instead of session/prompt (idle unchanged; also fixes the latent second-prompt-mid-turn bug). Local echo in the transcript (steer: <msg> — queued), updated when steering_consumed arrives. Toolbar chip while >=1 steer queued, cleared on consumed/cleared/turn-end. /steer command for the idle path. Advisory UI copy (the agent weighs the steer and can decline).
notes: Reconstructed 2026-06-17. Started immediately after K1a merged.

title: K1a steering non-functional: __session/steer double-underscore + dead inbound converter arm
description: prove-it-prototype (cyril-bm1j) against kiro-cli 2.8.0 found K1a steering broken end-to-end in both directions, missed by K1a unit tests (they bypass the real wire). (a) OUTBOUND: bridge.rs:1060/1127 call ExtRequest::new("_session/steer") but agent-client-protocol does format!("_{}",method) (lib.rs:213) -> wire shows __session/steer -> kiro -32601. Fix: pass "session/steer" / "session/steer/clear" (cf. SpawnSession's "session/spawn"). (b) INBOUND: echoes ride wire _kiro.dev/session/update; the lib strips the leading _ (lib.rs:294) so the converter sees kiro.dev/session/update; K1a's arm keyed "_kiro.dev/session/update" (convert/kiro.rs:681) is dead, echoes fall to the existing kiro.dev/session/update arm (line 424) and are dropped. Repro: .k1b-steering/probe.rs + wire_shim.py + oracle-wire-capture.log.
acceptance_criteria: A live mid-turn steer against kiro 2.7.0+ produces SteeringQueued in cyril (no -32601); a captured _kiro.dev/session/update steering echo yields SteeringQueued/Consumed/Cleared. Regression fence: wire-method test asserting the bytes are _session/steer (single _) and a converter test keyed on the stripped method.
notes: Closed: Fixed by PR #20 (merged ad71d4d): outbound bridge.rs now sends unprefixed session/steer[/clear] (single _ on wire); inbound steering echoes folded into the kiro.dev/session/update arm; dead _kiro.dev arm deleted. Verified end-to-end vs kiro 2.8.0 (.k1b-steering/fix-verification.log). Follow-up commit e4ac8e6 added the steer_methods_are_unprefixed regression fence.

title: dispatch_code_command Prompt path drops the message when busy (second cyril-2vcc path)
description: Code-review (cyril-bm1j, xhigh) found a SECOND instance of the cyril-2vcc bug class, outside the K1b diff so deferred. crates/cyril/src/app.rs dispatch_code_command's CodeCommandResponse::Prompt arm (app.rs:1152-1171) add_user_message()s + set_activity(Sending) + emits BridgeCommand::SendPrompt with NO busy guard. If a /code response yields a Prompt while session.status()==Busy, the post-cyril-84ca one-turn guard rejects the second SendPrompt with BridgeError -> the injected prompt is committed to chat but never sent, and activity regresses to Sending. cyril-2vcc fixed only submit_input (Enter-while-busy) via classify_submit->dispatch_steer; this /code prompt-injection path was not covered. FIX: gate this path on busy too (advisory or defer), mirroring the submit_input fix. Reachable when a user triggers a /code prompt-injection mid-turn.

title: Enter-while-busy silently drops the user's message (regression from cyril-84ca off-loop guard)
description:
Regression introduced by cyril-84ca (PR #22): pressing Enter (non-slash text) while session.status()==Busy silently drops the user's message.

ROOT CAUSE: app.rs submit_input() (crates/cyril/src/app.rs:640) has NO busy guard. On Enter it take_input()s (clears the box), add_user_message()s (commits the text to the visible chat), set_status(Busy)+set_activity(Sending), then sends BridgeCommand::SendPrompt. After cyril-84ca the bridge's one-turn guard rejects a second SendPrompt mid-turn with BridgeError{operation:'prompt', message:'a turn is already in progress'} (bridge.rs SendPrompt guard). So the message is committed to chat and the input cleared, but it is NEVER sent to the agent — only an error system message appears.

BEHAVIOR CHANGE: pre-cyril-84ca the blocked command loop QUEUED the second SendPrompt and ran it as a sequential second turn after the first completed (message eventually sent). Post-cyril-84ca it is rejected (message lost). Live on main in the window between cyril-84ca merging and cyril-bm1j (K1b) landing — cyril-84ca blocks cyril-bm1j, so this PR merges first.

SECOND AFFECTED PATH: dispatch_code_command's Prompt branch (app.rs:1043) programmatically emits SendPrompt with no busy check; a /code-triggered prompt arriving mid-turn is rejected the same way, and its already-committed user text is never sent.

UI INCOHERENCE: UiState's BridgeError handler (crates/cyril-ui/src/state.rs:638) only add_system_message()s — it does NOT clear activity. So set_activity(Sending) from the rejected submit regresses the spinner from Streaming/ToolRunning back to 'Sending', the user sees 'prompt failed: a turn is already in progress', and then turn 1 keeps streaming — a sequence that did not occur pre-cyril-84ca.

PROPER FIX: this IS 'the latent second-prompt-mid-turn bug' that cyril-bm1j (K1b) is designed to fix by routing Enter-while-busy to SteerSession when status==Busy and input is non-slash. If the pre-K1b window matters, a minimal interim guard in submit_input (don't commit/clear + show a 'turn in progress' hint, or drop the second send before mutating UI) closes it without the full K1b UX.

Found by /code-review of PR #22.
acceptance_criteria: Submitting non-slash text while session.status()==Busy must not silently drop the message: either route it to SteerSession (K1b) or, at minimum, leave the input uncleared/uncommitted and surface a clear 'turn in progress' affordance, with no orphaned activity state (no spinner regression to Sending, no BridgeError-only dead end). The /code Prompt path (app.rs:1043) must likewise not lose its injected prompt mid-turn. Regression test: with a Busy session, submit Enter and assert the message is not lost and activity/status stay consistent.

title: KAS-1 — Engine selection + _kiro/auth/getAccessToken responder (the entry gate)
description:
The precondition for every KAS milestone below. Without a host-supplied token, every KAS turn dies immediately ([TokenInvalidError] ... Host refresh callback returned no access token).

SCOPE (ROADMAP KAS-1, docs/ROADMAP.md:154):
- Make the KAS spawn real: the AgentEngine gate from KAS-0 (cyril-atjw) resolves the KAS engine to a live process; default stays v2. All new KAS code lands behind the 'kas' cargo feature from KAS-0.
  NOTE / CORRECTION: the engine flag was renamed 'kas' -> 'v3' as of kiro-cli 2.8.0 (probe-verified: 'kas' is rejected, possible values are v1/v2/v3; spawn is 'kiro-cli acp --agent-engine v3'). 2.7.1 accepted 'kas'. Resolve the engine string per installed version, don't hardcode 'kas'.
- Implement a '_kiro/auth/getAccessToken' server->client request responder: reply {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn}. KAS validates expiresAt > now + ~3 min and REQUIRES profileArn (backend 400s 'profileArn is required' without it).
- Token sourcing — MIRROR Kiro's own responder, don't just read the sqlite row. Kiro (crates/chat-cli-v2/src/auth/{kas_token,refresh_coordinator,social,builder_id,external_idp}.rs): (1) resolves the active token across THREE types — social (GitHub -> kirocli:social:token, carries profile_arn), AWS Builder ID, external IdP (kirocli:external-idp:token); (2) proactively OIDC-refreshes before the ~3-min pre-expiry buffer via a lock-guarded refresh_coordinator; (3) answers {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn}. A naive 'read kirocli:social:token and return it' responder breaks on the refresh buffer (the failure our probes hit) and on Builder-ID/external-IdP users. Cleanest: DELEGATE to kiro-cli's own auth rather than reimplement OIDC refresh + multi-type resolution.
- This makes cyril a CUSTODIAN of a kiro credential — handle as such (no logging, read-only, minimal lifetime). See ROADMAP Open Tension #7.

This is the AUTH half of the initialize._meta.kiro (KiroClientMeta) handshake; the SETTINGS half is cyril-nhzw (AgentSettings).

REFS: ROADMAP KAS-1 (docs/ROADMAP.md:154); covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md (KiroClientMeta handshake); memory reference_kiro_kas_launch_contract.
acceptance_criteria:
* Engine-select plumbing resolves the KAS engine to a live spawn (version-correct flag: v3 on >=2.8.0, kas on 2.7.1); default remains v2.
* '_kiro/auth/getAccessToken' responder replies {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn} and is exercised in a gated end-to-end smoke against a live KAS session.
* Auth-responder unit test covers the reply shape AND expired-token rejection (expiresAt <= now + ~3 min).
* profileArn is always present in the reply (Builder-ID / external-IdP users included), sourced by delegating to kiro-cli's own auth.
* Credential is never logged; token read is read-only and minimal-lifetime.

title: KAS-2b — Live context-usage breakdown bar (session_info_update:context_usage)
description:
On KAS (v3), the per-category context breakdown is PUSHED proactively on the wire — so cyril can render a live categorized context bar (Files / Tools / Prompts / Responses) in the toolbar WITHOUT the user running /context. On v1/v2 the breakdown only exists as the pulled /context command response; the streaming _kiro.dev/metadata notification there carries only the scalar contextUsagePercentage.

WIRE SHAPE (probe-verified, kiro-cli 2.8.0, 2026-06-19, live KAS turn):
- session/update -> session_info_update, _meta.kiro.kind == 'context_usage', payload {usagePercentage, breakdown?} (covenant ContextUsageBreakdown).
- breakdown = 5 buckets {contextFiles, tools, kiroResponses, yourPrompts, sessionFiles}, each {tokens, percent, items?}.
- ~6 frames pushed per turn.

CONSTRAINTS THAT SHAPE THE UI (all probe-verified):
1. tools bucket is AGGREGATE-ONLY — items is structurally absent (live: {tokens:33703, percent:3.4}). Only contextFiles/sessionFiles ever carry items[]. Do NOT build per-tool drill-down; the wire cannot support it on either engine.
2. breakdown is OPTIONAL PER FRAME — some context_usage frames carry usagePercentage only (breakdown absent). Retain last non-absent; treat absence as 'no update', not 'cleared' (same discipline as the effort field).
3. cyril's existing /context path (kiro.dev/commands/execute) is REJECTED by KAS (-32603 'no persistence classification'). Must read the streaming notification, not the command surface.

MAPPING: breakdown is richer than cyril's flat ContextUsage/TokenCounts — either map usagePercentage to ContextUsage + extend, or model the breakdown as a new type. The itemized buckets can reuse the existing render helper append_context_items (crates/cyril/src/app.rs:900), today wired only to the pulled /context path.

DEPENDS ON: KAS-2a (walking skeleton — turn renders+ends via session_info_update:turn_end; not yet filed). Part of the KAS-2 _kiro/* dialect track, anchored on KAS-0 (cyril-atjw).

REFS: probe experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-context-breakdown-2.8.0.py; ROADMAP KAS-2b (docs/ROADMAP.md:179).
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine, cyril renders a live categorized context bar fed by session_info_update:context_usage frames — no /context command issued.
* Per-frame 'breakdown absent' is handled: last breakdown retained, not cleared.
* tools bucket renders as an aggregate row (no per-tool UI); contextFiles/sessionFiles render itemized when items[] is present.
* v1/v2 engine behavior is unchanged (still uses the pulled /context command response).
* State + render tests cover (a) a breakdown-absent frame and (b) the aggregate-only tools bucket.

title: Bridge command loop blocks on conn.prompt() for the whole turn — no mid-turn commands (steer/cancel)
description: prove-it-prototype (cyril-bm1j) proved: bridge.rs awaits conn.prompt().await INSIDE the single-consumer command loop (while let Some(cmd)=command_rx.recv()), so it never recv()s a queued command until the turn ends. Wire oracle: SteerSession enqueued at +1.5s did not cross the wire until 12.839s, 1ms after the session/prompt response at 12.838s. Consequences: (1) mid-turn steering (K1b core) is impossible as-is; (2) STRONGLY IMPLICATED but not separately probed: Esc-while-busy CancelRequest goes through the same loop, so mid-turn cancel is also non-functional today. Fix direction: drive the prompt off the command loop (spawn_local the conn.prompt() future; resolve TurnCompleted from that task) so the loop stays free to process SteerSession/CancelRequest concurrently. Repro: .k1b-steering/probe.rs + oracle-wire-capture.log.
acceptance_criteria: A command enqueued mid-turn is sent to the agent before TurnCompleted (probe: steer surfaces before turn end). Esc-cancel aborts a busy turn promptly. Regression fence: an integration test driving the bridge with a slow turn + a mid-turn command, asserting the command's effect precedes turn completion.
notes: Implementation complete on fix/bridge-loop-midturn-commands (7 slices, checkpointed-build). Off-loop prompt dispatch: SendPrompt spawn_local's the turn on an Rc<conn>; one-in-flight guard via JoinHandle::is_finished; Shutdown aborts. Fences C1(loop frees) C2(steer mid-turn) C3(cancel resolves Cancelled) C4(single turn) C6(one TurnCompleted) C7(shutdown) C9(guard self-clears) all pass via an in-process fake-agent harness; 734 workspace tests + clippy + fmt clean; prove-it oracles (test_bridge, steer/cancel/notif_order probes) agree vs real kiro 2.8.0. C5 (ordering) resolved empirically — holds for v2 AND KAS, not a cyril-84ca property; theoretical zero-gap library race filed cyril-9akh. Ready for PR; unblocks K1b (cyril-bm1j).

title: Streamed agent text can render after TurnCompleted (ACP notification-vs-response ordering race)
description: agent-client-protocol (rpc.rs) resolves incoming RESPONSES inline in the read loop (oneshot send) but routes incoming NOTIFICATIONS through a separate incoming_tx queue handled by another task. So when a session/update notification immediately precedes the prompt response on the wire (zero scheduling gap), the response (-> TurnCompleted) can overtake the not-yet-handled notification on cyril's notification channel, committing TurnCompleted before the final streamed AgentMessage. Discovered during cyril-84ca Slice 7 via a zero-gap unit harness. NOT introduced by cyril-84ca (the pre-fix inline conn.prompt().await shares the identical race). Empirically does NOT manifest against real kiro 2.8.0 v2 OR KAS (.cyril-84ca/notif_order.rs: order is [msg,msg,TURN] for v2, [msg,TURN] for KAS) because real backends leave enough timing gap for the notification queue to drain. Low priority: theoretical/edge race, no observed production impact. Possible fixes if it ever surfaces: have the turn task yield/drain pending notifications before emitting TurnCompleted, or coalesce turn-end behind the notification queue.
acceptance_criteria: A unit/integration test reproduces the reorder deterministically AND a fix makes streamed AgentMessages always precede TurnCompleted on the notification channel even at zero wire-gap; verified to still hold against real v2 + KAS.

title: KAS-2a — Walking skeleton: a plain KAS turn renders and ends
description:
The milestone that earns a demo: select KAS, authenticate, prompt, watch text + tool calls stream, turn completes, input is live again. The MINIMUM for a plain turn to render and end cleanly. Other _kiro/* surfaces (governance, mcp/status, powers, progressive_context) and the other session_info_update kinds all fire on a plain turn too, but cyril's existing unknown-variant debug! arm drops them gracefully — deferred to 2b-2d without hanging anything.

SCOPE (ROADMAP KAS-2a, docs/ROADMAP.md:165):
- Converter arm (engine-keyed, in the Engine-trait structure from KAS-0) for agent_message_chunk / agent_thought_chunk / tool_call / tool_call_update ONLY.
- session_info_update -> kind:'turn_end' mapped to cyril's turn-completion / busy-clear. THIS — not the session/prompt response — is the turn-completion signal under KAS. HANG-PROOFING: cyril's prompt-response await must be NON-BLOCKING under KAS — drive completion off turn_end, treat the prompt response as secondary/whenever-it-arrives, so a non-returning response cannot freeze the skeleton. Verify with existing probe-kas-* scripts (no new probe needed).
- Answer _kiro/terminal/shell_type ({sessionId} -> {shellType: bash|zsh|fish|powershell|sh}) — lightest host callback, called once at session setup; missing reply yields 'Shell: undefined' in the system prompt.
- Handle sess_*-prefixed session ids and per-run log dirs under ~/.kiro/logs/<ts>/.
- Defensive unknown-variant tolerance for everything else (one debug! line noting _kiro/* surfaces are intentionally dormant pre-2b).

CRITICAL REWORK (ROADMAP note, docs/ROADMAP.md:177): the off-loop busy-guard + TurnCompleted emission from PR #22 (cyril-84ca) must be reworked here. That PR emits TurnCompleted when conn.prompt().await resolves and keys the one-turn busy-guard off prompt_task.is_finished() — both encode 'prompt response == turn end', correct for v2 but FALSE for KAS (turn-end is session_info_update->turn_end; the prompt response is secondary/late). Under KAS, is_finished() stays false after a logical turn-end (next prompt wrongly rejected 'a turn is already in progress') and TurnCompleted fires late/never — the exact hang the non-blocking criterion guards against. Fix: route BOTH the busy-guard and TurnCompleted through the engine's turn-end signal (KAS-0 turn-end detection in the Engine trait), not prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await. Concrete code: the SendPrompt guard + spawned prompt_task in bridge.rs.

REFS: ROADMAP KAS-2a (docs/ROADMAP.md:165); existing experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-*.py.
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine: select KAS, authenticate, send a prompt; agent text + tool calls stream and render, the turn completes, and input becomes live again.
* Turn completion is driven by session_info_update->turn_end, NOT the session/prompt response; the prompt-response await is non-blocking (a non-returning response cannot freeze the skeleton).
* The busy-guard and TurnCompleted emission are routed through the engine turn-end signal (not prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await); a second prompt after a logical turn-end is accepted, not rejected with 'a turn is already in progress'.
* _kiro/terminal/shell_type is answered (no 'Shell: undefined').
* sess_*-prefixed session ids handled; unknown _kiro/* variants produce a single debug! line, no hang.
* v1/v2 engine path unchanged.

title: KAS — Marshal kiro-cli settings into the _meta.kiro.settings (AgentSettings) handshake
description:
On KAS (v3), per-session feature flags ride the wire as AgentSettings in the initialize handshake: clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.settings (KiroClientMeta; covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md section 3, lines 98-104). cyril sends only {cwd, mcpServers} with NO _meta.kiro.settings, so on KAS EVERY feature flag is off: toolSearch (MCP token savings), thinking, knowledge, codeIntelligence, subagentOrchestration, compaction, todoList, checkpoint, semanticReview, fta, tangentMode, disableAutoCompaction, and the _c2s/_subagent/_delegate/_providerPowers/_parallelTasks/etc. family. KAS thus runs in a degraded all-flags-off mode.

The v2 TUI assembles this object from the kiro-cli settings file (function zme() in tui.js) and hands it to the ACP client constructor. cyril has no equivalent and no AgentSettings type (called out as a gap in covenant section 10).

CONTRAST WITH v2 (why this is KAS-only): the v2 Rust backend reads toolSearch (and other settings) DIRECTLY from the kiro-cli settings file across the ACP boundary — free for cyril, same pattern as trust-persistence. KAS is a separate Node process that cannot read the Rust settings store, so the client must marshal settings over the wire. See memory reference_kiro_tool_search_setting.

SCOPE: read kiro-cli settings (global + workspace) and build _meta.kiro.settings matching the AgentSettings shape, sent in the KAS initialize handshake. Default-sensitive flags (covenant section 3): semanticReview default ON if absent; fta default OFF; subagentOrchestration true -> orchestrate_subagent tool, false/absent -> invoke_sub_agent (CHANGES the subagent tool — directly affects KAS-3 rendering); toolSearch: {enabled, minPct, minTokens, neverDefer}; knowledge/compaction nested objects.

WHY IT MATTERS: gates MCP token savings via toolSearch (governs the tools-bucket size in cyril-5et2's context bar), thinking, and subagent-engine selection (KAS-3). Foundational enabler shared by multiple downstream KAS milestones.

DEPENDS ON: KAS-1 auth responder (KAS sessions must actually run first; not yet filed as its own issue) and the KAS-0 engine gate (cyril-atjw). This is the SETTINGS half of the same initialize._meta.kiro handshake whose AUTH half is KAS-1.

ROADMAP NOTE: the ROADMAP currently spells out only the auth half of the KAS-1 handshake; AgentSettings appears only piecemeal (subagentOrchestration at docs/ROADMAP.md:209). Recommend making AgentSettings explicit under KAS-1 (doc edit, separate from this ticket).

REFS: covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md section 3; memory reference_kiro_tool_search_setting; v2 TUI zme().
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine, cyril sends clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.settings in initialize, populated from kiro-cli settings (global + workspace) and matching the AgentSettings shape.
* toolSearch.{enabled,minPct,minTokens} is forwarded and observably takes effect on KAS (tool_search advertised, MCP tools deferred out of the loaded set).
* Default-sensitive flags handled correctly: semanticReview default-ON-if-absent, fta default-OFF, subagentOrchestration selects orchestrate_subagent vs invoke_sub_agent.
* A new AgentSettings type in cyril-core with serde camelCase, unit-tested for wire shape + the default-sensitive flags.
* v1/v2 engine path is unchanged (no _meta.kiro.settings sent or required there).
